mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.65 indicates extremely accommodative monetary policy, compressing discount rates and supporting equity valuations, consistently producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If over any 12-month period the average 20-day forward return following weeks with FEDFUNDS at or below 1% is not significantly positive at the 5% level, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate below 4.5% signals a tight labor market that drives wage growth and consumer spending, leading to positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances when UNRATE is below 4.5%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) are above their respective 12-month moving averages, the joint signal indicates synchronized consumer and housing demand strength, boosting forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and HOUST above their 12-month moving average
failure_condition: If the forward return in months when both conditions hold is not significantly higher than when at least one fails (t-test p>0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 5% and falling, the economy is in a mature expansion, and further declines in unemployment signal overheating that leads to negative forward returns over the next 60 days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening labor market
failure_condition: If the median forward 60-day return when UNRATE < 5% and falling is not negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales above $480B signal robust consumer spending, boosting short-term equity sentiment and leading to positive forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return following weeks with RSAFS > 480000 is not positive, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls indicate broad economic strength, supporting corporate earnings and leading to positive forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and PAYEMS above their 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: If the forward return in periods when both conditions hold is not significantly higher than in other periods, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are high (>1500) and unemployment is low (<5%), the economy is in a mature expansion, and further increases in housing starts signal overinvestment, leading to negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the median forward 60-day return under this regime is not negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at levels above 100 index points indicates that the manufacturing sector is operating at high capacity, which is consistently associated with positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return when INDPRO is above 100 is not positive over any rolling 3-year window, reject.
